message:
🐛 fix(e2e): persist resilience results to disk to avoid shared-state bug (#9002) (#9017)

The `report` object in `web/e2e/compliance/error-resilience.spec.ts` was
declared at module level and mutated by each of the five resilience tests,
with a final `generate report` test aggregating the results. Under
`fullyParallel: true` in `playwright.config.ts`, each worker loads its own
copy of the module, so if the tests ever get split across workers the
aggregator sees an incomplete (or empty) `report.checks` array.

While `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` currently keeps the
suite in a single worker, this is structural coincidence, not a guarantee —
any refactor that splits the describe (or the file-level parallel setting)
would silently corrupt the report.

Fix: replace the module-level singleton with per-test JSON files under
`test-results/error-resilience-partials/`. Each test writes its own result
in `addResult()`, and `generate report` aggregates by reading every JSON
file in that directory. A `beforeAll` clears stale partials so previous
runs can't bleed into the current aggregate.

This matches the fix pattern recommended in the issue (temp-file
aggregation) and is robust against worker splits.

Fixes #9002

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
